title: Quaqua cincta
taxonomy: Quaqua cincta is classified as a member of the plant family Apocynaceae. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to New Zealand. Quaqua cincta is a species of flowering plant endemic to the North Island of New Zealand. The plant's classification is further detailed in botanical research as a member of the Apocynaceae family.
properties: Quaqua cincta is a flowering plant native to South Africa, and while information on its therapeutic properties is limited, it has been traditionally used in folk medicine.
